---
title: "Fast Food Data Comparison"
---

**Repo:** https://github.com/Lucas-Gates/fast-food-analysis

### Problem
People compare fast food chains using opinions, but there’s a lot of measurable data:
- nutrition per item
- price/value
- reviews

### The Project
A data pipeline that collects and compares fast-food chains using multiple sources, then stores everything in a SQLite database for analysis.

- `collect_data.py` includes web scraping logic (menus/prices/calories) and external data collection
- `store_data.py` builds a normalized SQLite database (`final.db`) and loads datasets into tables
- `analyze_data.py` queries the DB and produces comparison plots (nutrition metrics, etc.)

### Key Features
- Multi-source ingestion (web + CSV + DB)
- Analysis driven by SQL queries
- Visual comparison outputs

### Skills demonstrated
- Web scraping (BeautifulSoup + requests)
- Database design + ETL into SQLite
- Analytics using SQL + Python
- Visualization and interpretation

### Example Visualizations
